allowedPathPatterns.reason: Migration inventory intentionally names enterprise-era concepts until this change removes or archives them.
Executable guardrail inventory intentionally lists blocked terms and current residual counts.
Historical handoff document that records previous enterprise-era implementation state.
Schema migration documentation must mention historical database columns while owner-scope migration is planned.
Flyway migrations preserve historical SQL column names for existing durable data.
Vendor metadata migrations document historical SQL column names.
Default-off compatibility adapters may translate enterprise-shaped requests during migration.
Diagnostics surfaces may expose historical migration state when not part of the default product path.
Persistence adapters retain historical database column and table names while exposing owner-scope service contracts.
Durable store adapters retain historical SQL columns and one-time legacy state-key reads for migration compatibility.
Legacy workbench views are hidden migration diagnostics and are not part of the personal Agent default UI.
Persistence migration tests verify historical schema compatibility and migration safety.
Session store compatibility test verifies migrated schemas still carry historical SQL columns for rollback safety.
Current OpenSpec change describes the removal scope and must mention the terms it removes.
Archived OpenSpec material is historical context, not the personal Agent product path.
The guardrail configuration must list the blocked terms and allowed historical paths.
